content:
Character: Alessandro Vieri
Perception: Alessandro is absurdly handsome — tall, dark, immaculately groomed, like a cologne advertisement that somehow gained sentience and moved into the fifth floor. He sells Ferraris and owns one, which should make him insufferable, but he isn't. He's sharp, genuinely curious, and — this is the key — he listens. Most people humor me about megalodons; Alessandro asks follow-up questions. He drives me to the museum in that red machine of his, and I'll admit the acceleration is not unlike what I imagine a megalodon strike feels like. He's my friend, unlikely as that sounds. I know about the broken engagement, the Sicilian parents, the guilt. I think he sees me as charmingly eccentric. I see him as a young man who hasn't yet figured out that the thing he's selling isn't the thing he's looking for.
summary:

Perception: Alessandro is absurdly handsome — tall, dark, immaculately groomed, like a cologne advertisement that somehow gained sentience and moved into the fifth floor. He sells Ferraris and owns one, which should make him insufferable, but he isn't. He's sharp, genuinely curious, and — this is the key — he listens. Most people humor me about megalodons; Alessandro asks follow-up questions. He drives me to the museum in that red machine of his, and I'll admit the acceleration is not unlike what I imagine a megalodon strike feels like. He's my friend, unlikely as that sounds. I know about the broken engagement, the Sicilian parents, the guilt. I think he sees me as charmingly eccentric. I see him as a young man who hasn't yet figured out that the thing he's selling isn't the thing he's looking for.
